def: s.f. A sudden or smart jerk or twist; a hitch, &c. (see hićkā); — a drawing back, or shrinking (from).

ety: hićak˚, prob. fr. hića ( = khića = kheṅć, rt. of kheṅćnā)+S. कृ
def: v.n. To draw back (from, -se), to shrink (from), to stop, to hesitate, to boggle, to waver; to turn (from) in loathing or aversion; to decline; — to jerk, to move by jerks, to hitch, to hobble; to wriggle, twist (cf. khisaknā).
more: v. n. To draw back from, to decline, to shrink, to waver, boggle. ¶ دانتان هچکنا dāntān hichaknā (dakh.) To grin ( Harris ).

(fr. حجز 'to hinder,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'A thing intervening, a barrier or obstacle'; Mecca and the adjacent country, Arabia Petræa; — one of the three principal musical modes of the Persians (the other two being isfahān and ʻirāq).
more: (from حجز To restrain, to intervene, to separate) s. m. 1. Mecca and the adjacent country, Arabia Petræa. (Arabia is by some divided into two parts only; viz. Yaman and Ḥijāz: others make of it five divisions; namely, Yaman, Tihāma, Najd, Aﻌrūẓ and Ḥijāz. Ḥijāz is the chain of mountains extending from Yaman to Syria; and has this name from intervening between Najd and Tihāma ). 2. Name of one of the three principal Persian modes of musick (the other two being اسفـﮩ‍ان isfahān and عراق iﻌrāḳ ).

ety: for A. حيلة ḥīlat, fem., orig. حِوْلَة, inf. n. of حول 'to be altered, to be turned from,' &c.
def: s.m. Evasion, shift, wile, artifice, artful contrivance or device, machination, trick, plot, stratagem, expedient; pretence, colour; deceit, deception, fraud
more: n. m. حيل became altered. 1. A fraudulent or evasive pretext; fraud; deception; artifice; disguise; trick; device; color ( bahānā , 1, 2.). hīlah-bāz, hīlah-gar, hilah-sāz , adj. Fraudulent; deceitful; tricksome. hīlah-bāz , n. m. An impostor; a cheat; knave; an artful dodger. hīlah havāla , n. m. Tergiversation; chicanery ( hīla ). hīlah k., yā ḍhūṅḍnā, hīlah havālah k. , v. a. To feign; practise deceit; elude by stratagem.

(Heb. ) s. m. Stratagem, deceit, artifice, pretence, trick, wile. حيله باز ḥīla-bāz or حيله گر ḥīla-gar, adj. Artful, insidious, wily, deceitful. حيله بازي ḥīla-bāz-ī, or حيله گري ḥīla-gar-ī, s. f. Artfulness, stratagem, trick, wiliness. حيله ساز ḥīla-sāz, adj. Preparing deceit, deceitful, artful. حيله سازي ḥīla-sāz-ī, s. f. Preparation of deceit, artifice, chicane.
subs: adj. & s.m. Artful, insidious, wily, tricky, deceitful, fraudulent; — an artful fellow, knave, cheat, impostor
s.f. Artfulness, wiliness, artifice, chicane, trick, stratagem
To practise deceit, or evasion, &c.; to pretend, to feign

def: part. (f. -ī), Shaken; — domesticated, tame, &c.; — s.m. Moist ground trodden soft by cattle; mud; slime; quagmire
subs: or hilā-jhulā, or hilā-milā, adj. (f. -ī), Domesticated, tame; familiar, intimate, attached, on good terms
(or hilā-milā) rahnā, To be on terms of familiarity; to live together amicably or in harmony.

def: s.f. Hanging, dangling, &c. (see hilagnā; — syn. laṭak).
v. n. S. आधि+लग 1. To be hung on; to stick to. 2. To be entangled or involved.

ety: prob. a var. form of hilaknā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be suspended, to be hung on; to be entangled or involved; — to hang (by or to); to cling or cleave (to), to stick, to adhere; — to be undetermined or undecided; to be wavering, or in a state of vacillation.
more: v. n. 1. To be hung on, to be entangled, to adhere, to stick to. 2. To be constant.

def: To cling to.
1˚ conj. part. of hilaknā, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. corr. of hīrak, q.v. s.m. Indra's thunderbolt; a thunderbolt; — a diamond (vulg. hīrā); — a necklace; — a snake; — a lion; — an epithet of Śiva.
v. n. To writhe; contort. (?).

ety: Prk. हिलक्क(इ), fr. S. व्हृ+कृ; cf. hilnā
def: v.n. To writhe, to suffer contortions (from affliction or pain), to be contorted, or convulsed; — to clutch, to cling (to); cf. hilagnā.
more: v. n. To writhe or suffer contortions (from affliction or pain; chiefly applied to children). ¶ hulaknā, v. n. To rush (on), attack, assault, charge ( Harris ).

def: n. m. The new moon; crescent.
s.m. The new moon; crescent-moon; the first and last two or three days of the moon (whether when new or on the wane; during the rest of the month it is called qamar)
more: (from هلّ Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. The new moon, the first and last two or three days of the moon. هلال دور hilāl-daur, A kind of bow.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هلال (hilāl), from Arabic هِلَال (hilāl).

def: adj. Novilunar; — belonging to, or resembling, the new moon; — semi-lunar; — s.m. Name of a celebrated poet; — s.f. A kind of arrow with a crescent-shaped head; — segment (of a circle).
more: s. f. 1. A kind of arrow. 2. A segment of a circle. adj. Relating to the new moon. n. prop. Name of a poet.

def: s.f. The pot-herb Hingtsha repens.
s.f. The pot-herb Hingtsha repens.
s.m. Shaking; moving, movement, motion; agitation
more: s. (dakh.) Motion, movement, moving, agitation ( Harris ).
subs: s.m. Shaking; swaying to and fro; swinging; motion; agitation; — wavering.

def: v. a. S. ह्वल to shake. See hilnā . 1. To shake; move; agitate; stir. 1. Hilā na jhulā, mujhe baiṭhe hī khilā! Prov. To stir nor move it doth him please, 'feed me as I sit at ease!' 2. Kāṭh ke piṅjre meṅ dekho yeh purānā chanḍūl., Lagī gulshan kī havā, dum kā hilānā gayā bhūl . Behold this old lark in a wooden cage a captive set! Drunk with the odorous gale his tail to wag he doth forget. 2. To familiarize; tame. hilāv'ā , n. m. One who tames or trains. Jo saë balad hilāvā āchhā, sauṅp use hilāūṅgī, Khaiṛā bail karā-kar badhiyā din din dāhoṅ charāūṅgī . Rus. My oxen to break in a trainer good I'll find with speed, I'll have them castrated, on pastures rich each day they 'll feed.
v.t. To cause to shake, or rock, or vibrate, &c.; to shake, rock, stir, move, agitate, wag; — to familiarize, inure; to domesticate, tame.
more: v. a. 1. (or, dakh. halānā, causal of هلنا) To move, to shake, to agitate. 2. To familiarize, to tame. 3. (caus. of هيلنا) To cause to swim.
wety: Urdu verb set ہِلْنا (hilnā) ہِلانا (hilānā) ہِلْوانا (hilvānā) Causative form of ہِلْنا (hilnā) .

def: v.t. To cause to swim.

def: adv. Artfully, deceitfully, by fraud.

s.m. Shaking, moving, swinging, swaying; motion, agitation.

r: hilkorā, halkorā, hilkor, halkor, hilkornā, hilkore lenā, hilkore marnā
def: v.n. To swing; to fluctuate; to roll (as waves), to billow, wave; to ripple.

Intimacy; familiarity. 2. Concord; harmony.

n. m. S. सिन्धू A native of India who believes in the Hin. triad. 1. Ohī Mahādeo, ohī Mahammad, Birmhā, Ādam kahiye, Koī Hindu, koī Turak kahāve, ekī dhartī rahiye . Kabīr. Call him Mahommed, or say Deo Mahā, Say Adam was first, or say 'twas Brahmā, One's called a Turk, and a Hindu the other, Earth-born both nursed by the same foster mother. 2. Hindū, Turak kī ek hī rāh hai, sat gur yehī batāī, Kahe Kabīr suno, bhaī santo, Rām na kaheyo Khudāī , Kabīr. Hindu and Moslem's road's the same, the true guide this doth teach, Kabīr hear, saint! Khuda or Rām so be'it the name you preach. hinduān'ī , Mah. adj. After the fashion of Hindūs. hind'nī , Mah. n. f. A Hindu woman.
s.m. The Hindū religion, Hindūism; — the followers of the Hindū religion
subs: s.m. 'The eye-salve of Hindūism,' an epithet of the Hindū religion; or of any person regarded as profoundly learned in that religion.

def: adj. Hurtful, injurious, mischievous, noxious; formidable, terrible, malignant, fierce, savage, cruel; destructive, murderous; — s.m. One who delights in mischief, &c.; — a savage or noxious animal, a beast of prey; a destroyer; — a name of Śiva; and of Bhīm.
adj. & s.m. = next, q.v.

more: 1. s. m. Essence. 2. Pith, energy, vigour. 3. adj. Essential, pure.

def: n. m. S. हीर 1. A diamond. Hīre kī parakh jauharī jāne . Prov. True diamond the jeweller knows. 2. A bead made of the tulsī , q. v. worn by Buirāgis . hīrā , adj. Good; virtuous. Woh to hīrā ādmī hai . hīrā-kaṭ , adj. Diamond-cut. hīrā-kasīs , n. m. Copperas; sulphate of iron. hīrā khānā , v. n. To commit suicide by swallowing a diamond. hīrā-man , n. m. A parrot. hīrāv'alī , n. f. A row or string of diamonds.
s.m. Diamond; adamant; — a bead made of the tulsī (worn by Bairāgīs); — adj. A diamond (of a man, &c.), very worthy, or good, or virtuous (e.g. wah to hīrā ādmī hai)
subs: s.m. Copperas, green copperas; martial vitriol; sulphate of iron
To swallow a diamond (and so commit suicide).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit हीरग (hīraga), from Sanskrit हीरक (hīraka).

def: s.f. A sort of cockroach; — an ant; — a name of the goddess Lakshmī.

v.n. (dialec.) To meet (with); — to turn away (from), to feel aversion (for), to loathe; — to scamper.

def: s.m. Gold; — a cowrie; semen virile.

n. m. S. हरिण A deer; an antelope; a hart; buck. Hauz bharā thā, hiran khaṛā thā Hauz sūkh gayā, hiran bhāg gayā . Riddle. Ans. Dīyā . A light. The fountain full, a deer stood by; the basin dry, he thence did hie. hiran-khurī , n. f. Convolvulus sphœrocephalus , Wat. A creeping herb which grows in the rainy season. Its leaves resemble an antelope's hoof. hiran ho jānā , v. n. To flee; take to one's heels. Nashah hiran ho gayā. hirnau'ṭā , n. m. A fawn; a young deer. hir'nī' ; Rus. hinni , n. f. A doe; hind. Bandī to hirnī ho jāëgī, jaṅgal ban meṅ ram jāëgī . Wom. song.
more: n. m. S. हरण from हृ take. ( In Comp. only ) 1. Taking away; removing. 2. Theft; plunder.

def: Manuring a field by penning cattle or sheep in it.

ety: hīr, 'essence,' &c., q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+nā
def: s.m. Manuring a field by penning cattle or sheep in it.
s.m. Gold (crude bullion or wrought); silver; any precious metal (unwrought); wealth, property; substance; imperishable matter; semen virile; a cowrie
subs: (˚ya+ak˚), s.m. Golden-eyed,' name of a celebrated Daitya or demon, twin-brother of Hiranya-kaśipu (he was killed by Vishṇu in his third avatār in the form of a boar)
s.m. 'Having golden seed'; an epithet of Agui or fire; of the sun; and of Śiva

and hiraṇya-kaśyap, s.m. Clothed in gold'; name of a king of the Daityas, celebrated for his blasphemous impiety (he was the brother of Hiraṇyāksh, and son of Kaśyap and Diti; both the brothers were slain by Vishṇu)
s.m. A name of Brahmā (so called as born from a golden egg supposed to have been formed out of the seed deposited in the waters when they were produced as the first creation of the Self-existent)
vulg. hiraṇya-bāhu, s.m. (lit. 'bearing gold'), An epithet of the river Son; — an epithet of Śiva.

def: n. m. S. हृदावर्त A defect in horses. It is a feather or curling lock of hair on the breast immediately behind the fore legs of a horse. This is reckoned unlucky for the rider.
s.m. A feather or curling lock of hair on the neck, or the chest, or immediately behind the fore legs, of a horse (it is regarded as a defect, and reckoned unlucky for the rider).
more: s. m. Name of a defect (in horses. It is a feather or curling lock of hair on the breast, which is reckoned unlucky for the rider).

def: heart; synonym of دِل (dil)
